items.prompt: What does dataset documentation primarily capture for reuse?
choices: Context such as structure, content, and rationale for creation and processing
GPU brand and cloud region only
A single accuracy number from one experiment
items.explain: Documentation supplies the contextual metadata needed to interpret and reuse data, not just a headline metric.

items.prompt: Without documentation, even the original dataset creators may forget processing decisions that later analyses depend on.
items.explain: Example 8.1 emphasizes that missing notes harm both newcomers and original authors.

items.prompt: Example 8.2 (feature drift) shows that version control mainly helps teams:
choices: Inspect when a feature entered and roll back if it harms performance
items.explain: History enables diagnosis and rollback when feature changes degrade models.

items.prompt: Why does clinical research motivate dataset change tracking?
items.rubric: Answer should mention regulatory compliance, auditability, or mandated controls over trial data changes.
items.sample_answer: Regulators such as the FDA require controlled, auditable tracking of changes to clinical-trial data.

items.prompt: Version control for datasets supports collaboration by reducing silent overwrites and enabling merges of concurrent work.
items.explain: Structured tracking and merge workflows keep shared datasets coherent across contributors.
